title: Instruction-Based Image Editing
concept: Models trained on (original image, instruction, edited image) triples let users edit photos using plain language commands like 'make it snow'.

exercises.prompt: If you could edit a photo just by typing a sentence, what would you type?

options.text: Learn 50 buttons in a photo app first
Nothing, photos can't be changed by words

exercises.explain: That's exactly the idea! New AI tools let you just describe the change you want in plain words, like 'make it snow', and the photo changes.

cards.title: Just Say What You Want
cards.body: Imagine typing 'make it snow' under a photo of a park... and suddenly it's snowing in the picture! No editing tools, no sliders. Just words. That's instruction-based image editing, and it's real.

cards.title: What Is Instruction-Based Image Editing?
cards.body: It's when an AI model changes a photo based on a plain-language command you type, like 'add a rainbow' or 'make it night time'. The AI understands your words and edits the picture to match, without you touching any editing tools.

cards.title: Like a Magic Genie for Photos
cards.body: Think of a genie who can change your picture the moment you say a wish out loud. You don't need to know how the genie's magic works—you just say 'make it sunset' and it happens. The AI model is your photo genie, trained to turn your words into real changes.

cards.title: From Day to Sunset
cards.body: You upload a photo of a beach at noon and type: 'make it look like sunset'. The AI edits the sky to orange and pink tones, adds warm lighting, and keeps the beach and people exactly the same. One instruction, one edited photo.

cards.title: Myth: The AI Just Adds a Filter
cards.body: It's not just a colored overlay like a simple filter! The AI actually understands the meaning of your instruction and changes specific parts of the image (like the sky or weather) while leaving the rest untouched, something a basic filter can't do.

cards.title: Old Editing vs. Instruction Editing
cards.body: See the difference between manual photo editing and telling an AI what you want.

left.text: Click menus, drag sliders, learn tools to change brightness, sky color, or weather by hand

right.text: Type 'make it snow' and the AI edits the photo for you automatically

exercises.prompt: What does an instruction-based image editing AI use to make changes to a photo?

options.text: A plain-language command typed by the user
A list of editing tool buttons

exercises.explain: The AI reads your written instruction, like 'make it snow', and figures out how to change the image to match what you asked for.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Instruction-based image editing models are trained using examples of an original image, an instruction, and the edited result.
exercises.explain: True! These models learn from many triples of (original photo, instruction, edited photo) so they can learn the pattern of how instructions change images.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Instead of clicking editing tools, you can type a simple ___ like 'make it snow' to change a photo.
exercises.explain: The word 'instruction' fits because it means a plain command telling the AI what change to make to the photo.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the best example of an instruction-based image edit?

options.text: Typing 'add a rainbow to the sky' and the AI adds one
Manually painting a rainbow pixel by pixel in an art program
Taking a completely new photo that already has a rainbow

exercises.explain: Instruction-based editing means using words to tell the AI what to change, and it edits the existing photo for you, unlike manual painting or taking a new picture.

exercises.prompt: You have a photo of your backyard in summer. You want it to look like winter. What should you do with an instruction-based image editing AI?

options.text: Type an instruction like 'make it look like winter with snow'
Manually redraw the entire backyard from scratch
Wait for real winter and take a new photo
Change the file name of the photo to 'winter.jpg'

exercises.explain: The whole point of instruction-based editing is that you just describe the change in plain words, like 'make it look like winter with snow', and the trained AI model edits the photo for you.
